summary: Fragment-only shader swap with config gating is a clean, minimal-surface-area change. The plan correctly preserves the vertex shader, reuses existing uniforms, and gates the feature behind quality tiers.
strengths: Fragment-only change — vertex shader (noise3d + pointWarp.vert) is untouched, eliminating varying/attribute mismatch risk
Correctly identifies that uBassEnergy, uTrebleEnergy, and uTime already exist in the uniform block; only uVoronoiGridSize is new
Edge tint via RGB darkening avoids color-space round-trip mismatch with the vertex shader's palette-derived vColor
Quality-tier gating (disabled on low) respects the existing performance scaling pattern
Self-contained hash-based voronoi (fract/sin/dot) avoids importing noise3d into the fragment shader, keeping the shader modular
Test plan includes shader content assertions (checking for F2, gl_PointCoord, absence of snoise/fbm3) — not just 'does it init'

concerns.issue: Step 0 is unnecessary — working tree is clean (git status shows no changes). Executing 'git checkout HEAD -- src/ tests/' on a clean tree is harmless but adds noise.
concerns.suggestion: Remove step 0 or make it conditional on dirty working tree detection.

concerns.issue: The current fragment shader is imported as a module-level const: `import fragmentShader from '../shaders/pointWarp.frag.glsl?raw'`. The plan's step 3 says to 'select fragmentShader' at init() time but doesn't explicitly address the naming collision with the existing top-level import.
concerns.suggestion: Rename the existing import to `pointWarpFrag` (or similar) and import voronoiFragShader alongside it, then select in init().

concerns.issue: The circular discard (dist > 0.5 from gl_PointCoord) in the voronoi shader clips the voronoi pattern to a circle per point. This is consistent with the existing soft-circle shader but means voronoi cells will be visible only within each point's circular footprint, not as a continuous tiled pattern across the screen.
concerns.suggestion: This is actually the correct approach for GL_POINTS rendering — each point is an independent quad. The stained-glass effect emerges from many overlapping points. No change needed, but the plan could note this explicitly to set visual expectations.

architecturalImpact.shaderPipeline: Adds one new fragment shader file and a conditional selection path in pointCloud.ts. The vertex shader chain is unchanged. Impact is additive-only.
architecturalImpact.qualitySystem: Adds one boolean field (enableVoronoiCells) to QualityProfile. Follows the existing pattern of feature flags gated by tier.
architecturalImpact.configSurface: Adds one optional boolean (useVoronoiShader) to PointCloudConfig. Follows existing optional config pattern (enableSparkle, enablePointerRepulsion, etc.).
architecturalImpact.uniformBlock: Adds one new uniform (uVoronoiGridSize). All other uniforms consumed by the voronoi shader already exist.
architecturalImpact.testSurface: Creates the first dedicated point cloud test file. Uses the same patterns established by electricArc.test.ts.
architecturalImpact.breakingChanges: None. Default behavior (useVoronoiShader: false / undefined) produces identical output to current state.

dependencyCheck.US-041: Required — provides the point cloud system, ShaderMaterial, and uniform block that this plan extends
dependencyCheck.US-048: Required — provides quality tier system and QualityProfile interface that this plan adds enableVoronoiCells to

acceptanceCriteriaCoverage.Shader compiles cleanly with no WebGL er: partially-covered — build gate validates TypeScript/bundling but GLSL compile errors are runtime-only; ShaderErrorCollector file is referenced but not yet implemented
